monitors.description: The monitors that are in an account.

nextToken.description: The token for the next set of results. You receive this token from a previous call.

monitorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the monitor.

monitorStatus.description:
The status of a monitor. The status can be one of the following

  • PENDING: The monitor is in the process of being created.

  • ACTIVE: The monitor is active.

  • INACTIVE: The monitor is inactive.

  • ERROR: Monitor creation failed due to an error.

  • DELETING: The monitor is in the process of being deleted.

nextToken.description: The token for the next set of results. You receive this token from a previous call.

scopeId.description: The identifier for the scope that includes the resources that you want to get data results for. A scope ID is an internally-generated identifier that includes all the resources for the accounts in a scope.

status.description:
The status for a scope. The status can be one of the following: SUCCEEDED, IN_PROGRESS, FAILED, DEACTIVATING, or DEACTIVATED.

A status of DEACTIVATING means that you've requested a scope to be deactivated and Network Flow Monitor is in the process of deactivating the scope. A status of DEACTIVATED means that the deactivating process is complete.

scopeArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the scope.

monitorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the monitor.

monitorStatus.description:
The status of a monitor. The status can be one of the following

  • PENDING: The monitor is in the process of being created.

  • ACTIVE: The monitor is active.

  • INACTIVE: The monitor is inactive.

  • ERROR: Monitor creation failed due to an error.

  • DELETING: The monitor is in the process of being deleted.

localResources.description: The local resources to monitor. A local resource in a workload is the location of hosts where the Network Flow Monitor agent is installed.

remoteResources.description: The remote resources to monitor. A remote resource is the other endpoint specified for the network flow of a workload, with a local resource. For example, Amazon Dynamo DB can be a remote resource.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the monitor was created.

modifiedAt.description: The last date and time that the monitor was modified.

type.description: The type of the local resource. Valid values are AWS::EC2::VPC AWS::AvailabilityZone, AWS::EC2::Subnet, AWS::EKS::Cluster, or AWS::Region.

identifier.description:
The identifier of the local resource. The values you can specify are the following:

  • For a VPC, subnet or EKS cluster, this identifier is the VPC Amazon Resource Name (ARN), subnet ARN or cluster ARN.

  • For an Availability Zone, this identifier is the AZ name, for example, us-west-2b.

  • For a Region, this identifier is the Region name, for example, us-west-2.

scopeId.description: The identifier for the scope that includes the resources you want to get metrics for. A scope ID is an internally-generated identifier that includes all the resources for a specific root account.

status.description:
The status for a scope. The status can be one of the following: SUCCEEDED, IN_PROGRESS, FAILED, DEACTIVATING, or DEACTIVATED.

A status of DEACTIVATING means that you've requested a scope to be deactivated and Network Flow Monitor is in the process of deactivating the scope. A status of DEACTIVATED means that the deactivating process is complete.

scopeArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the scope.

monitorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the monitor.

monitorStatus.description:
The status of a monitor. The status can be one of the following

  • PENDING: The monitor is in the process of being created.

  • ACTIVE: The monitor is active.

  • INACTIVE: The monitor is inactive.

  • ERROR: Monitor creation failed due to an error.

  • DELETING: The monitor is in the process of being deleted.

localResources.description: The local resources to monitor. A local resource in a workload is the location of the hosts where the Network Flow Monitor agent is installed.

remoteResources.description: The remote resources to monitor. A remote resource is the other endpoint specified for the network flow of a workload, with a local resource. For example, Amazon Dynamo DB can be a remote resource.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the monitor was created.

modifiedAt.description: The date and time when the monitor was last modified.

type.description: The type of the local resource. Valid values are AWS::EC2::VPC AWS::AvailabilityZone, AWS::EC2::Subnet, AWS::EKS::Cluster, or AWS::Region.

identifier.description:
The identifier of the local resource. The values you can specify are the following:

  • For a VPC, subnet or EKS cluster, this identifier is the VPC Amazon Resource Name (ARN), subnet ARN or cluster ARN.

  • For an Availability Zone, this identifier is the AZ name, for example, us-west-2b.

  • For a Region, this identifier is the Region name, for example, us-west-2.

unit.description: The units for a metric returned by the query.

topContributors.description: The top contributor network flows overall for a specific metric type, for example, the number of retransmissions.

nextToken.description: The token for the next set of results. You receive this token from a previous call.

localIp.description: The IP address of the local resource for a top contributor network flow.

snatIp.description: The secure network address translation (SNAT) IP address for a top contributor network flow.

localInstanceId.description: The instance identifier for the local resource for a top contributor network flow.

localVpcId.description: The VPC ID for a top contributor network flow for the local resource.

localRegion.description: The Amazon Web Services Region for the local resource for a top contributor network flow.

localAz.description: The Availability Zone for the local resource for a top contributor network flow.

localSubnetId.description: The subnet ID for the local resource for a top contributor network flow.

destinationCategory.description:
The destination category for a top contributors row. Destination categories can be one of the following:

  • INTRA_AZ: Top contributor network flows within a single Availability Zone

  • INTER_AZ: Top contributor network flows between Availability Zones

  • INTER_REGION: Top contributor network flows between Regions (to the edge of another Region)

  • INTER_VPC: Top contributor network flows between VPCs

  • AWS_SERVICES: Top contributor network flows to or from Amazon Web Services services

  • UNCLASSIFIED: Top contributor network flows that do not have a bucket classification

remoteVpcId.description: The VPC ID for a top contributor network flow for the remote resource.

remoteRegion.description: The Amazon Web Services Region for the remote resource for a top contributor network flow.

remoteAz.description: The Availability Zone for the remote resource for a top contributor network flow.

remoteSubnetId.description: The subnet ID for the remote resource for a top contributor network flow.

remoteInstanceId.description: The instance identifier for the remote resource for a top contributor network flow.

remoteIp.description: The IP address of the remote resource for a top contributor network flow.

dnatIp.description: The destination network address translation (DNAT) IP address for a top contributor network flow.

value.description: The value of the metric for a top contributor network flow.

traversedConstructs.description: The constructs traversed by a network flow.

localServiceName.description: The service name for a local resource.

localPodName.description: The name of the pod for a local resource.

localPodNamespace.description: The namespace of the pod for a local resource.

remoteServiceName.description: The service name for a remote resource.

remotePodName.description: The name of the pod for a remote resource.

remotePodNamespace.description: The namespace of the pod for a remote resource.

localSubnetArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a local subnet.

localVpcArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a local VPC.

remoteSubnetArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a remote subnet.

remoteVpcArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a remote VPC.

topContributors.description: The top contributor network flows overall for a specific metric type, for example, the number of retransmissions.

nextToken.description: The token for the next set of results. You receive this token from a previous call.

accountId.description: The account ID for a specific row of data.

localSubnetId.description: The subnet identifier for the local resource.

localAz.description: The identifier for the Availability Zone where the local resource is located.

localVpcId.description: The identifier for the VPC for the local resource.

localRegion.description: The Amazon Web Services Region where the local resource is located.

remoteIdentifier.description: The identifier of a remote resource. For a VPC or subnet, this identifier is the VPC Amazon Resource Name (ARN) or subnet ARN. For an Availability Zone, this identifier is the AZ name, for example, us-west-2b. For an Amazon Web Services Region , this identifier is the Region name, for example, us-west-2.

localSubnetArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a local subnet.

localVpcArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a local VPC.

unit.description: The units for a metric returned by the query.

nextToken.description: The token for the next set of results. You receive this token from a previous call.

timestamps.description: An array of the timestamps for the data point.

status.description:
When you run a query, use this call to check the status of the query to make sure that the query has SUCCEEDED before you review the results.

  • QUEUED: The query is scheduled to run.

  • RUNNING: The query is in progress but not complete.

  • SUCCEEDED: The query completed sucessfully.

  • FAILED: The query failed due to an error.

  • CANCELED: The query was canceled.

When you run a query, use this call to check the status of the query to make sure that the query has SUCCEEDED before you review the results.

  • QUEUED: The query is scheduled to run.

  • RUNNING: The query is in progress but not complete.

  • SUCCEEDED: The query completed sucessfully.

  • FAILED: The query failed due to an error.

  • CANCELED: The query was canceled.

The status of a query for top contributors data.

  • QUEUED: The query is scheduled to run.

  • RUNNING: The query is in progress but not complete.

  • SUCCEEDED: The query completed sucessfully.

  • FAILED: The query failed due to an error.

  • CANCELED: The query was canceled.

scopeId.description: The identifier for the scope that includes the resources you want to get data results for. A scope ID is an internally-generated identifier that includes all the resources for a specific root account. A scope ID is returned from a CreateScope API call.

status.description:
The status for a scope. The status can be one of the following: SUCCEEDED, IN_PROGRESS, FAILED, DEACTIVATING, or DEACTIVATED.

A status of DEACTIVATING means that you've requested a scope to be deactivated and Network Flow Monitor is in the process of deactivating the scope. A status of DEACTIVATED means that the deactivating process is complete.

scopeArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the scope.

targets.description: The targets to define the scope to be monitored. A target is an array of targetResources, which are currently Region-account pairs, defined by targetResource constructs.

targetIdentifier.description: A target identifier is a pair of identifying information for a scope. A target identifier is made up of a targetID (currently always an account ID) and a targetType (currently always an account).

targetId.description: The identifier for a target, which is currently always an account ID .

accountId.description: The identifier for the account for a target.

targetType.description: The type of a target. A target type is currently always ACCOUNT.

region.description: The Amazon Web Services Region for the scope.

queryId.description: The identifier for the query. A query ID is an internally-generated identifier for a specific query returned from an API call to start a query.
The identifier for the query. A query ID is an internally-generated identifier for a specific query returned from an API call to start a query.

The status of a monitor. The status can be one of the following

  • ERROR: Monitor creation failed due to an error.

  • DELETING: The monitor is in the process of being deleted.

localResources.description: The local resources to monitor. A local resource in a workload is the location of hosts where the Network Flow Monitor agent is installed.

remoteResources.description:
The remote resources updated for a monitor, as an array of resources with identifiers and types.

A remote resource is the other endpoint specified for the network flow of a workload, with a local resource. For example, Amazon Dynamo DB can be a remote resource.

modifiedAt.description: The last date and time that the monitor was modified.

type.description: The type of the local resource. Valid values are AWS::EC2::VPC AWS::AvailabilityZone, AWS::EC2::Subnet, AWS::EKS::Cluster, or AWS::Region.

identifier.description:
The identifier of the local resource. The values you can specify are the following:

  • For a VPC, subnet or EKS cluster, this identifier is the VPC Amazon Resource Name (ARN), subnet ARN or cluster ARN.

  • For an Availability Zone, this identifier is the AZ name, for example, us-west-2b.

  • For a Region, this identifier is the Region name, for example, us-west-2.

status.description:
The status for a scope. The status can be one of the following: SUCCEEDED, IN_PROGRESS, FAILED, DEACTIVATING, or DEACTIVATED.

A status of DEACTIVATING means that you've requested a scope to be deactivated and Network Flow Monitor is in the process of deactivating the scope. A status of DEACTIVATED means that the deactivating process is complete.
